extra.reasoning:
The invariant "opted_out implies not sent" is an inductive invariant preserved by every transition, so it holds on all reachable executions regardless of length.

Initial state: opted_out=false, sent=false -> antecedent false -> invariant holds.

Examine each transition's post-state:
- t_optout (on optout, always): sets opted_out=true AND sent=false simultaneously. Consequent (not sent) is true -> holds.
- t_send_allowed (on send_attempt, guard not opted_out): sets sent=true but leaves opted_out=false. Antecedent false -> holds.
- t_send_blocked (on send_attempt, guard opted_out): sets sent=false. Consequent true -> holds.
- t_tick (on tick, always): sets sent=false (and bumps ticks). Consequent true -> holds regardless of opted_out.
- No enabled transition: state unchanged; invariant carries over by induction.

Key structural argument: sent can only be set true by t_send_allowed, which is guarded by not opted_out and does not modify opted_out — so when sent becomes true, opted_out is false. The only transition that sets opted_out=true (t_optout) forces sent=false in the same step. There is no transition that makes opted_out=true while leaving sent=true, and none that sets sent=true while opted_out is already true. Hence the dangerous state (opted_out=true AND sent=true) is unreachable. The 6-event bound is not binding because preservation is unconditional. Property holds.
